questions.text: The magnetic field lines around a straight current-carrying conductor are:
questions.explanation: Magnetic field lines around a straight current-carrying wire form concentric circles with the wire at the center.

questions.text: Fleming's left-hand rule is used for electric motors.
questions.explanation: Fleming's left-hand rule gives the direction of force on a current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field (motor principle).

questions.correctAnswer: The production of electric current due to changing magnetic field.
questions.explanation: Electromagnetic induction is the phenomenon where a changing magnetic field induces an electric current in a conductor.

questions.text: The device used to convert mechanical energy to electrical energy is called ________.
questions.explanation: A generator (dynamo) converts mechanical energy into electrical energy using electromagnetic induction.
